perturbation.original_text:
Year 1 - Due: December 31, 2018 - $50,000
Year 2 - Due: December 31, 2019 - $75,000
Remaining Balance Due: March 30, 2020 - $25,000
perturbation.changed_text:
Year 1 - Due: December 31, 2018 - $50,000
Year 2 - Due: June 30, 2019 - $75,000
Remaining Balance Due: March 30, 2020 - $25,000
perturbation.explanation: Changing the payment due date in 'Attachment A. E. Relationship Structure & Payment Schedule' introduces an in-text contradiction regarding when the payments are due. This creates uncertainty and disputes over when Freeze Tag, Inc. is contractually obligated to provide the sponsorship funds.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
E. Relationship Structure & Payment Schedule
Company agrees to pay to the Association the cash rights fee in the amount of $150,000 for this Sponsorship Agreement. Payments to Association shall be payable according to the following schedule:
Year 1 - Due: December 31, 2018 - $50,000
Year 2 - Due: June 30, 2019 - $75,000
Remaining Balance Due: March 30, 2020 - $25,000
Signatures: American Diabetes Association Freeze Tag, Inc.
By: By:
Name Name
Title Title
Date Date
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text alters the payment due date for Year 2 from 'December 31, 2019' to 'June 30, 2019'. The Contradicted Text, which is identical to the original text, specifies the original due date of 'December 31, 2019'. This creates a direct conflict within the document regarding when the Year 2 payment is due, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines'.

perturbation.original_text: Before expiration of the Term, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon: (i) any material breach of the Agreement by the other Party, if such breach is not remedied to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party within ten (10) business days after written notice; (ii) ten (10) business days written notice to the other Party whenever the notifying Party in its sole discretion determines that the continuation of the Agreement will damage its reputation or good will; or (iii) written notice in the event one Party (a) becomes or is declared insolvent or bankrupt or is subject to the appointment of a trustee or receiver or any equivalent thereof, (b) is the subject of any proceeding related to its liquidation or insolvency (whether voluntary or involuntary) which is not dismissed within ninety (90) days, (c) makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or (d) is subject to any sale, lease or other transfer of all or substantially all of its assets to any entity; or (e) is subject to a change of control (whether by merger, stock transfer or otherwise), except in the case of an initial public offering.
perturbation.changed_text: Before expiration of the Term, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon: (i) any material breach of the Agreement by the other Party, if such breach is not remedied to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party within thirty (30) business days after written notice; (ii) ten (10) business days written notice to the other Party whenever the notifying Party in its sole discretion determines that the continuation of the Agreement will damage its reputation or good will; or (iii) written notice in the event one Party (a) becomes or is declared insolvent or bankrupt or is subject to the appointment of a trustee or receiver or any equivalent thereof, (b) is the subject of any proceeding related to its liquidation or insolvency (whether voluntary or involuntary) which is not dismissed within ninety (90) days, (c) makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or (d) is subject to any sale, lease or other transfer of all or substantially all of its assets to any entity; or (e) is subject to a change of control (whether by merger, stock transfer or otherwise), except in the case of an initial public offering.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the 'remedy period' for a material breach from 'ten (10)' to 'thirty (30) business days' in Section 14 creates an in-text contradiction. While subsection (i) allows 30 days to remedy a breach, subsection (ii) allows termination with only ten (10) days notice if reputation or goodwill is damaged. This inconsistency could lead to disputes about which termination clause takes precedence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Termination: Before expiration of the Term, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon: (i) any material breach of the Agreement by the other Party, if such breach is not remedied to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party within thirty (30) business days after written notice; (ii) ten (10) business days written notice to the other Party whenever the notifying Party in its sole discretion determines that the continuation of the Agreement will damage its reputation or good will; or (iii) written notice in the event one Party (a) becomes or is declared insolvent or bankrupt or is subject to the appointment of a trustee or receiver or any equivalent thereof, (b) is the subject of any proceeding related to its liquidation or insolvency (whether voluntary or involuntary) which is not dismissed within ninety (90) days, (c) makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or (d) is subject to any sale, lease or other transfer of all or substantially all of its assets to any entity; or (e) is subject to a change of control (whether by merger, stock transfer or otherwise), except in the case of an initial public offering.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'remedy period' for a material breach from 'ten (10)' to 'thirty (30) business days' in Section 14(i). This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 14(ii) still allows termination with only ten (10) business days' notice if reputation or goodwill is damaged. This inconsistency leads to a direct conflict, as a party has 30 days to cure a material breach but could be terminated with only 10 days' notice under subsection (ii) for reputational damage, which could stem from that same breach. The contradictory text is found in Section 14: 'Before expiration of the Term, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon: (i) any material breach of the Agreement by the other Party, if such breach is not remedied to the reasonable satisfaction of the non-breaching Party within thirty (30) business days after written notice; (ii) ten (10) business days written notice to the other Party whenever the notifying Party in its sole discretion determines that the continuation of the Agreement will damage its reputation or good will.'

perturbation.original_text: All other originals and copies of the Association Marks (whether in printed, electronic, recorded, and/or other tangible form) shall be discarded or destroyed within five (5) business days.
perturbation.changed_text: All other originals and copies of the Association Marks (whether in printed, electronic, recorded, and/or other tangible form) shall be discarded or destroyed within thirty (30) business days.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the time frame for discarding or destroying the Association Marks from 'five (5)' business days to 'thirty (30)' business days, after Termination or Expiration creates an in-text contradiction. Section 15 states different discarding dates than the original, and this change leads to disputes about the length of time a company has before they have to discard them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. Effect of Termination or Expiration: Upon termination or expiration, no further use may be made of the Association Marks, or other proprietary property or materials provided, developed or intended for use in connection with the Sponsorship, without prior written authorization, other than as set out in this section. All other originals and copies of the Association Marks (whether in printed, electronic, recorded, and/or other tangible form) shall be discarded or destroyed within thirty (30) business days. The obligations under sections 8, 9, 10, and 11 and 19 and this section 14 shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change in the discarding timeframe from 'five (5)' business days to 'thirty (30)' business days directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly states that 'All other originals and copies of the Association Marks (whether in printed, electronic, recorded, and/or other tangible form) shall be discarded or destroyed within thirty (30) business days.' This creates an inconsistency regarding the required timeframe for discarding the Association Marks, leading to potential disputes about compliance with the agreement, meeting the conditions for 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
